mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity relative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window in zero-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 500K persons signals robust labor market momentum and consumer spending capacity. This drives positive equity returns over 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Correlation between month-over-month PAYEMS growth and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (Pearson r < 0.15) over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth combined with housing starts (HOUST) growth above trend indicates broad-based consumer and construction demand. Joint strength in these series signals sustained economic expansion and positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 1% AND HOUST > 1200K units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 439,771M and HOUST at 1,089K as of 2020-07-15
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with synchronized consumer and housing strength
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when both RSAFS and HOUST conditions are met versus when only one or neither is met, over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential margin compression. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings and growth expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS still below 1% signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression. This interaction produces positive equity returns over 20 trading days as real borrowing costs decline and asset prices inflate.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 236.15 and FEDFUNDS at 0.11% as of 2020-07-15
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when both inflation and low-rate conditions are met versus when only one is met, over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth rate above 2% annualized signals sustained economic expansion. This always-on trend mechanism produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations remain elevated.
regime_hint: any regime with positive trend growth
failure_condition: Correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth rate and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (Pearson r < 0.20) over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals synchronized supply and labor expansion. This interaction produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as production capacity and workforce both strengthen.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 200K
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.51 and PAYEMS at 140,849K as of 2020-07-15
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when both INDPRO and PAYEMS growth conditions are met versus when only one or neither is met, over any 12-month rolling window.
